intro: Tissa paradoxa, also known as Tissa or Indian Tissa, is a plant species that belongs to the family Annonaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tissa paradoxa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
